titlestmt.author: by Diana M. Smith

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday November 6, 2018 at 04:02

PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[JOHN L. DE FOREST / SHOLEM ASCH COLLECTION ])//EN" "asch.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

did.unittitle: John L. De Forest / Sholem Asch collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Collection consists of correspondence addressed to Asch, many letters carrying dictated drafts of his response on them. There are four typescripts of essays by Sholem Asch as well as five scripts for a Christmas pageant performed in Stamford, Conn., in 1943. Also present is De Forest's typescript memoir entitled, "My Hours with Sholem Asch."

persname.part: De Forest, John L. (John Le Roy), 1916-

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The John L. De Forest / Sholem Asch Collection was given by John L. De Forest in 1993.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The John L. De Forest / Sholem Asch Collection are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John L. De Forest / Sholem Asch Collection. General Collection. Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John L. De Forest acquired these papers while serving as Sholem Asch's secretary. The bulk of the collection consists of correspondence addressed to Asch, many letters carrying dictated drafts of his response on them. There are four typescripts of essays by Sholem Asch as well as five scripts for a Christmas pageant performed in Stamford, Connecticut, in 1943. Also present is De Forest's typescript memoir (ca. 1991) entitled "My Hours with Sholem Asch."

persname.part: De Forest, John L. (John Le Roy), 1916-

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Asch to Congregation Rodeph Sholom
